rag_rules: Insert AGREE text only as an `annex agree {** ... **};` block owned by a valid AADL component type or component implementation.
Type-scope contracts over the component interface belong in the component type; implementation-only bindings belong in the implementation.
Use the input AADL model as the authority for valid owners, declared interface elements, subcomponents, and connections during fusion.
generic_rules: Choose the narrowest owner scope that legally exposes the referenced symbols.
Preserve existing architecture and non-annex content; fusion should add the annex without changing unrelated declarations or structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Keep the inserted text as a syntactically complete `annex agree {** ... **};` section.
When fusing into an implementation, place the annex in a location consistent with the surrounding subcomponent and connection sections.
anti_patterns: Do not place a type-level annex that directly depends on subcomponent ports or other implementation-only names.
Do not rename elements, reorder architecture sections unnecessarily, or edit unrelated properties while inserting the annex.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
